name: Bill Crow
abstract: William Orval Crow (born December 27, 1927) is an American jazz bassist. Among other work, Crow was the long-term bassist in saxophonist Gerry Mulligan's bands in the 1950s and 1960s.

interviews.title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: And then my main trio after a couple of months was Bill Crow and Joe Morello.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: finally arrived at this good trio with Bill Crow and Joe Morello. That was the . . . one of the good groups I had in there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was Mel Lewis, bass was Bill Crow and the piano was John Bunch.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: George Masso
about_quotes.evidence: I know Bill Crow very well. I just spoke with Bill on the phone two days ago.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Masso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Crow was in the band, it was great but I knew I was in fast company…
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Stan Getz, with Duke Jordan and Bill Crow on bass.
